pageSubtitleV2: Assessment based on OECD indicators and risk model v0.6.5
heroBadgeLabel: Based on {{count}} international indicators · OECD, IMF, World Bank
metricRiskSubtitle: of all federal contracts analyzed show patterns consistent with corruption — flagged by our AI model
metricValueSubtitle: in government contracts since 2002 — every peso tracked and evaluated
metricCasesSubtitle: confirmed corruption cases used to train and validate the risk detection model
metricCasesNote: IMSS, Segalmex, COVID procurement, and more
bullet2: The most at-risk sectors are {{sectors}} — where competition is lowest and vendor concentration is highest.
bullet3: Direct awards (contracts given without a public bid) account for a large portion of total spending, reducing accountability.
sectorSubtitle: Darker red = more contracts flagged as high risk in that sector. Click a sector for details.
trendTitle: Is it getting better or worse?
methodologyNote: This report uses the Procurement Health Index (PHI) and the RUBLI AI risk model (v0.6.5). Grades reflect competition rates, single-bidder tenders, vendor concentration, and external red flags. All data comes from COMPRANET, Mexico's official procurement registry.
sourcesLabel: Sources: COMPRANET · OECD Procurement Benchmarks · IMF CRI · SAT EFOS Registry

computingDetail: This may take a moment. Refreshing automatically...

gradeMethodology.body: Grade based on 5 dimensions: direct award rate, vendor concentration, critical risk rate, threshold-splitting indicators, and data coverage. OECD recommends <25% direct awards — Mexico averaged 79.4%.
